title: The Rosette Nebula in Narrowband
explanation: A vast cloud of glowing hydrogen surrounds a young cluster of hot stars in the constellation Monoceros. Stellar winds have carved out the central cavity, and the surrounding gas fluoresces in the ultraviolet light of the newborn suns.

title: A Comet in the Morning Sky
explanation: A long ion tail streams away from a comet low over the eastern horizon before dawn. Swept in from the outer solar system, it is brightening as it nears the Sun.

title: The Horsehead Nebula in Infrared
explanation: The famous dark silhouette of the Horsehead glows softly when viewed in infrared light, revealing structure hidden behind the obscuring dust of Orion.

title: The Milky Way Over a Ridge Line
explanation: The bright core of our galaxy rises above a dark treeline just before first light. Clear, dry air makes the faint dust lanes stand out against the star clouds of Sagittarius.

title: Messier 81 and Messier 82
explanation: Two very different galaxies share a single field of view. The grand spiral M81 and the starburst galaxy M82 are locked in a gravitational dance in the constellation Ursa Major.

title: A Flight Through the Carina Nebula
explanation: A short visualization built from telescope data soars through towering pillars of gas and dust in one of the sky's largest star-forming regions.

title: Zodiacal Light After Sunset
explanation: A faint pyramid of light stretches up from the western horizon after twilight, sunlight scattered by dust spread along the plane of the solar system.

title: Sunspot Group AR3925 Up Close
explanation: A high-resolution view of a complex sunspot group shows dark umbral cores surrounded by the feathery filaments of the penumbra.

title: The Andromeda Galaxy in Wide Field
explanation: Our nearest large galactic neighbor spans an area of sky several times wider than the full Moon. Long exposures reveal faint outer spiral arms and the two bright companion galaxies flanking its glowing core.

title: Aurora Over a Frozen Lake
explanation: Green and violet curtains of aurora ripple above a still, ice-covered lake in northern latitudes. A burst of solar activity days earlier drove the display far south of its usual range.

title: The Pleiades Star Cluster
explanation: Blue reflection nebulosity wraps around the hot young stars of the Pleiades, one of the closest and most recognizable open clusters in the night sky.

title: Equinox Sunrise Over the Bay
explanation: On the day of the equinox the Sun rises due east, its light stretching straight across the water. Day and night stand nearly equal across the whole planet.

title: Jupiter and Its Great Red Spot
explanation: Swirling bands of cloud and the centuries-old storm known as the Great Red Spot dominate this sharp amateur image of the solar system's largest planet.

title: The Lagoon Nebula in Hydrogen and Oxygen
explanation: Bright ridges of ionized gas and dark dust lanes cut across this star-forming region in Sagittarius, glowing in the light of hydrogen and oxygen.

title: A Partial Solar Eclipse Sequence
explanation: A composite of frames follows the Moon as it takes a bite out of the Sun during a partial eclipse, the dark limb sliding slowly across the solar disk.

title: The Whirlpool Galaxy and Companion
explanation: The graceful spiral arms of M51 sweep around toward a smaller companion galaxy, their gravitational interaction triggering new waves of star formation.

title: Noctilucent Clouds at Twilight
explanation: Thin, electric-blue clouds glow near the edge of space long after sunset, lit by sunlight from far below the horizon.

title: The Orion Nebula from a Backyard Telescope
explanation: The nearest large stellar nursery blazes in the sword of Orion, its glowing gas sculpted by the radiation of the young Trapezium stars at its heart.

title: Saturn and Its Rings at Opposition
explanation: Near opposition the rings of Saturn appear especially bright as sunlight reflects straight back toward Earth, revealing the fine structure of the Cassini Division.

title: The Veil Nebula Supernova Remnant
explanation: Delicate filaments of glowing gas mark the expanding shock wave of a star that exploded thousands of years ago in the constellation Cygnus.
